commands.summary: New 'hardens webhook signatures (proxy-aware) and the local API surface' test: a signature computed against the local URL is rejected, a public-URL signature passes even with hostile X-Forwarded-Host/Host headers, a host-forged signature is rejected, private /api routes require the bearer token, media path-traversal attempts never return 200, and /api/config-status is redacted to booleans. 10/10 server tests pass.

Secret scanner unit tests (3/3): scanText flags private keys, AWS keys, assigned Twilio auth tokens, and generic secret assignments; ignores clean source; scanFiles detects a planted secret and skips node_modules and *.test.js.
Repo-wide secret scan over git-tracked files reports clean (no committed secrets), exercising the CLI end-to-end.
Renderer interaction tests + all node tests (incl. security + lifecycle) + node --check of all entry modules green.

environment.process_note: PR-011 security verification: proxy-aware webhook signature tests (validation binds to the configured public URL, not the request Host), local API threat tests (auth required, media traversal rejected, redacted config status), a git-tracked secret scanner (scan:secrets) with unit tests, a production dependency audit (scan:deps), and diagnostic redaction coverage. The webhook tunnel hardening posture (decision 0003 / 016 AGH-023) is reinforced by these tests.
